items.md: ### 0. FOREWORD
items.value: 0. FOREWORD

items.md: **0.1** This Indian Standard ( Part I ) ( First Revision ) was adopted by the Indian Standards Institution on 29 September 1982, after the draft finalized by the Code of Practice for Power Installation and Maintenance Sectional Committee had been approved by the Electrotechnical Division Council.
items.value: 0.1 This Indian Standard ( Part I ) ( First Revision ) was adopted by the Indian Standards Institution on 29 September 1982, after the draft finalized by the Code of Practice for Power Installation and Maintenance Sectional Committee had been approved by the Electrotechnical Division Council.

items.md: **0.2** It is essential that safety should be preached and practised at all times by all concerned in installation, operation and maintenance of electric lines and apparatus. The real benefit to be derived from a guide of this nature will be realized only when the safety instructions it contains are regarded as normal routine duty and not as involving extra and laborious operations.
items.value: 0.2 It is essential that safety should be preached and practised at all times by all concerned in installation, operation and maintenance of electric lines and apparatus. The real benefit to be derived from a guide of this nature will be realized only when the safety instructions it contains are regarded as normal routine duty and not as involving extra and laborious operations.

items.md: **0.3** This guide gives general instructions regarding safety procedures and practices to be followed in all major electrical installations. Special precautions are to be taken in mines and other hazardous areas where explosive or flammable gases are present. Guidelines for the same are provided in a subsequent part of the standard ( under preparation ). For precautions to be observed while installing, operating or maintaining any particular electrical equipment, reference shall also be made to the relevant Codes of Practice.

items.md: **0.4** This Indian Standard was originally published in 1969. The present revision is being brought out to introduce new concepts in the safety practices in electrical work and also to make the guide comprehensive in all respects as far as safety in electrical installations are concerned. # 1. SCOPE
items.value: 1. SCOPE

items.md: **1.1** This standard ( Part I ) gives recommendations regarding safety procedures and practices which should be followed to the extent applicable in all major electrical installations, such as generating stations, sub-stations, industrial establishments, transmission and distribution lines, and cable networks.
items.value: 1.1 This standard ( Part I ) gives recommendations regarding safety procedures and practices which should be followed to the extent applicable in all major electrical installations, such as generating stations, sub-stations, industrial establishments, transmission and distribution lines, and cable networks.

items.md: **1.2** For special precautions to be taken in mines and other hazardous areas, *see* Part IV of this standard.
items.value: 1.2 For special precautions to be taken in mines and other hazardous areas, see Part IV of this standard.

items.md: # 2. STATUTORY REGULATIONS
items.value: 2. STATUTORY REGULATIONS

items.md: **2.1** It is the duty of all persons who may be concerned with the installation, operation and maintenance of electric lines and apparatus to make themselves thoroughly conversant with the regulations and safety rules governing the work they may have to undertake on these lines and apparatus.
items.value: 2.1 It is the duty of all persons who may be concerned with the installation, operation and maintenance of electric lines and apparatus to make themselves thoroughly conversant with the regulations and safety rules governing the work they may have to undertake on these lines and apparatus.

items.md: **2.2** Generation, transmission, distribution and utilization of electrical energy in our country is governed by Indian Electricity Supply Act, 1948, Indian Electricity Act, 1910 and the Rules made thereunder, that is, Indian Electricity Rules, 1956.
items.value: 2.2 Generation, transmission, distribution and utilization of electrical energy in our country is governed by Indian Electricity Supply Act, 1948, Indian Electricity Act, 1910 and the Rules made thereunder, that is, Indian Electricity Rules, 1956.

items.md: **2.3** The electricity supply undertakings are required to comply with other statutory acts besides those given in **2.2**, and these shall be taken into account.
items.value: 2.3 The electricity supply undertakings are required to comply with other statutory acts besides those given in 2.2, and these shall be taken into account.

items.md: **2.4** In addition to the statutory regulations mentioned in **2.2** and **2.3**, electric supply undertakings usually lay down safety instructions in the form of safety rules or standing orders for the guidance of the staff employed in connection with the execution of work on or near the electric lines and apparatus and for their operation and maintenance which should be strictly complied with at all times.
items.value: 2.4 In addition to the statutory regulations mentioned in 2.2 and 2.3, electric supply undertakings usually lay down safety instructions in the form of safety rules or standing orders for the guidance of the staff employed in connection with the execution of work on or near the electric lines and apparatus and for their operation and maintenance which should be strictly complied with at all times.

items.md: **2.5** It shall be the responsibility of the person-in-charge to interpret and explain correctly the rules and regulations to all staff concerned and to ensure that the staff thoroughly understands the same.
items.value: 2.5 It shall be the responsibility of the person-in-charge to interpret and explain correctly the rules and regulations to all staff concerned and to ensure that the staff thoroughly understands the same.

items.md: **2.6** For the purpose of this standard, a competent person is one who had been certified so by the State Government, for the specific category of electrical work, as laid down in Rule 45 of the IE Rules 1956.
items.value: 2.6 For the purpose of this standard, a competent person is one who had been certified so by the State Government, for the specific category of electrical work, as laid down in Rule 45 of the IE Rules 1956.

instructions are issued by the competent authority to follow other procedures. In extenuating circumstances, such as for the purpose of saving life or time in the event of an emergency, it may become necessary to start the work without being able to obtain the necessary permit-to-work; in such cases, the action taken shall be reported to the person-in-charge as soon as possible. The permit-to-work certificate from the person-in-charge of operation to the person-in-charge of the men selected to carry out any particular work ensures that the portion of the installation where the work is to be carried out is rendered dead and safe for working. All work shall be carried out under the personal supervision of a competent person. If more than one department is working on the same apparatus, a permit-to-work should be issued to the person-in-charge of each department.

items.md: ### 5. SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS FOR WORKING ON LOW AND MEDIUM VOLTAGE MAINS AND APPARATUS
items.value: 5. SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS FOR WORKING ON LOW AND MEDIUM VOLTAGE MAINS AND APPARATUS

items.md: **5.1 Work on Dead Low and Medium Voltage Mains and Apparatus** — Unless a person is authorised to work on live low and medium voltage mains and apparatus, all mains and apparatus to be worked upon shall be isolated from all sources of supply before starting the work, proved dead, earthed and short-circuited. For earthing and short-circuiting, only recognized methods should be used. Measures shall be taken against the inadvertent energizing of the mains and apparatus.
items.value: 5.1 Work on Dead Low and Medium Voltage Mains and Apparatus — Unless a person is authorised to work on live low and medium voltage mains and apparatus, all mains and apparatus to be worked upon shall be isolated from all sources of supply before starting the work, proved dead, earthed and short-circuited. For earthing and short-circuiting, only recognized methods should be used. Measures shall be taken against the inadvertent energizing of the mains and apparatus.

items.md: **5.2 Work on Live Low and Medium Voltage Mains and Apparatus** — Only competent, experienced and authorized persons shall work on live mains and apparatus, and such persons should take all safety measures as may be required under the Indian Electricity Rules, 1956.
items.value: 5.2 Work on Live Low and Medium Voltage Mains and Apparatus — Only competent, experienced and authorized persons shall work on live mains and apparatus, and such persons should take all safety measures as may be required under the Indian Electricity Rules, 1956.

items.md: **5.3 Testing of Mains and Apparatus with Low Voltage** — No person shall apply test voltage to any mains unless he has received a permit-to-work and has warned all persons working on the mains of the proposed application of test voltage. If any part which will thus become alive is exposed, the person-in-charge of the test shall take due precautions to ensure that the exposed live portion does not constitute danger to any person. It should also be ensured before the application of test voltage, that no other permit-to-work has been issued for working on this mains.

items.md: **5.4 Connecting Dead Mains to Live Mains** — When dead mains are connected to live mains, all connections to the live parts shall be made last, and in all cases the phase sequence should be checked to ensure that only like phases are connected together. **6.4.2** High voltage contact indicator and phasing rods are provided for phasing and proving exposed high voltage mains and apparatus dead. A set consists of two rods connected in series by a length of insulated cables. Both rods are fitted with contact tips and indicating tubes. When the contact tip of one rod is applied to exposed live high voltage parts and that of the other to earth or other exposed live high voltage parts provided there is sufficient voltage difference between the two, the indicating tubes should glow. Each set of rods is normally marked for the maximum voltage on which it may be used and shall not, under any circumstances, be used on higher voltages.

items.md: **6.4.3** *Use of High Voltage Contact Indicator and Phasing Rods* — While using the high voltage contact indicator and phasing rods for proving the high voltage mains or apparatus dead, following precautions should be taken:
items.value: 6.4.3 Use of High Voltage Contact Indicator and Phasing Rods — While using the high voltage contact indicator and phasing rods for proving the high voltage mains or apparatus dead, following precautions should be taken:

items.md: a) Ensure that the rod is clean and dry;
items.value: a) Ensure that the rod is clean and dry;

items.md: b) Check the rod by applying it to known live parts of the correct voltage, the indicating tube shall glow;
items.value: b) Check the rod by applying it to known live parts of the correct voltage, the indicating tube shall glow;

items.md: c) Apply the rod to each phase required to be proved dead, the indicating tube shall not glow. Be very careful to be in a position to see the glow, if any, appearing in the indicating tube; and
items.value: c) Apply the rod to each phase required to be proved dead, the indicating tube shall not glow. Be very careful to be in a position to see the glow, if any, appearing in the indicating tube; and

items.md: d) Again check the rod by applying it to live parts as in (b) above, again the indicating tubes shall glow.
items.value: d) Again check the rod by applying it to live parts as in (b) above, again the indicating tubes shall glow.

items.md: **NOTE 1** — All the above operations shall be carried out at the same place and at the same time if no live high voltage parts are available on the site; rods up to 11 kV may be tested by applying them to the top of the spark plug in a running motor car engine. If the rod is in order, the indicating tube will glow each time the plug sparks. Therefore, the glow will be intermittent, but the indicating tube should glow on this test or the rod is useless as a means of proving the mains or apparatus dead.

### 6.4.6 *Earthing and Short-Circuiting High Voltage Mains*
items.value: 6.4.6 Earthing and Short-Circuiting High Voltage Mains

items.md: **6.4.6.1** High voltage mains shall not be worked upon unless they are discharged to earth after making them dead and are earthed and short-circuited with earthing and short-circuiting equipment adequate to carry possible short-circuit currents and which are specially meant for the purpose. All earthing switches wherever installed should be locked up.
items.value: 6.4.6.1 High voltage mains shall not be worked upon unless they are discharged to earth after making them dead and are earthed and short-circuited with earthing and short-circuiting equipment adequate to carry possible short-circuit currents and which are specially meant for the purpose. All earthing switches wherever installed should be locked up.

items.md: **6.4.6.2** If a high voltage cable is required to be cut, a steel wedge shall be carefully driven through it at the point where it is to be cut or preferably by means of a spiking gun of approved design.
items.value: 6.4.6.2 If a high voltage cable is required to be cut, a steel wedge shall be carefully driven through it at the point where it is to be cut or preferably by means of a spiking gun of approved design.

items.md: **6.4.6.3** After testing the high voltage cable with dc voltage, the cable shall be discharged through a 2 megohms resistance and not directly, owing to dielectric absorption which is particularly prominent in the dc voltage testing of high voltage cables. The cable shall be discharged for a sufficiently long period to prevent re-building up of voltage.

The conductor shall not be touched after the earthing device has been removed from it.

items.value: d) In cases where duplicate feed is available, both the circuit opening devices are in series between the mains and apparatus to be worked on and any source of supply.

items.md: e) In cases where duplicate feed is not available, the circuit opening device is between the mains to be worked on and any source of supply.
items.value: e) In cases where duplicate feed is not available, the circuit opening device is between the mains to be worked on and any source of supply.

items.md: **6.4.9.1** The circuit opening devices mentioned in **6.4.9** shall be locked in the open position before the work on the mains and apparatus is commenced. The locking devices shall be removed only by a competent person and not until the work has been completed, any short-circuiting and earthing removed and the permit-to-work form duly returned and cancelled.
items.value: 6.4.9.1 The circuit opening devices mentioned in 6.4.9 shall be locked in the open position before the work on the mains and apparatus is commenced. The locking devices shall be removed only by a competent person and not until the work has been completed, any short-circuiting and earthing removed and the permit-to-work form duly returned and cancelled.

items.md: **6.4.10** *Work on High Voltage Mains with Two or More Sections* — When the mains to be worked upon are to be divided into two or more sections, the provisions of **6.3**, **6.4.6** and **6.4.9** shall be observed with regard to each section.
items.value: 6.4.10 Work on High Voltage Mains with Two or More Sections — When the mains to be worked upon are to be divided into two or more sections, the provisions of 6.3, 6.4.6 and 6.4.9 shall be observed with regard to each section.

items.md: **6.5** In addition to the general safety instructions provided above, further specific guidelines from the safety point of view applicable for installation and maintenance work on individual electrical equipment are given in individual Codes of Practice. These shall be taken note of as appropriate.

# 7. WORKMEN'S SAFETY DEVICES AND APPLIANCES
items.value: 7. WORKMEN'S SAFETY DEVICES AND APPLIANCES

items.md: ## 7.1 General
items.value: 7.1 General

items.md: **7.1.1** Rubber gauntlets, gloves, mats, boots and galoshes, insulated platforms and stools, safety belts, hand lamps, tower wagons and other special insulated devices shall be used, as required, for working on electrical equipment and apparatus as precaution against accidental electric shock.
items.value: 7.1.1 Rubber gauntlets, gloves, mats, boots and galoshes, insulated platforms and stools, safety belts, hand lamps, tower wagons and other special insulated devices shall be used, as required, for working on electrical equipment and apparatus as precaution against accidental electric shock.

items.md: **7.1.2** Tools insulated with brittle material or otherwise liable to have its insulation damaged when in use, shall be used carefully. If the insulation has been damaged, these tools shall not be used.
items.value: 7.1.2 Tools insulated with brittle material or otherwise liable to have its insulation damaged when in use, shall be used carefully. If the insulation has been damaged, these tools shall not be used.

items.md: **7.1.3** The person-in-charge of the work should ensure proper maintenance and use of the safety equipment.
items.value: 7.1.3 The person-in-charge of the work should ensure proper maintenance and use of the safety equipment.

items.md: **7.1.4** *Durability of Rubber Devices* — As rubber perishes rapidly in tropical climate, great care shall be exercised in seeing that only sound rubber gauntlets, gloves, mats, boots, galoshes and other safety devices depending upon rubber insulation are issued to employees for working on live apparatus.
items.value: 7.1.4 Durability of Rubber Devices — As rubber perishes rapidly in tropical climate, great care shall be exercised in seeing that only sound rubber gauntlets, gloves, mats, boots, galoshes and other safety devices depending upon rubber insulation are issued to employees for working on live apparatus.

items.md: ## 7.2 Care of Rubber Gauntlets, Gloves, Etc
items.value: 7.2 Care of Rubber Gauntlets, Gloves, Etc

items.md: **7.2.1** No person should put his rubber gauntlets or gloves into his coat or trouser pocket along with other tools. Gauntlets carried in this way are liable to get damaged and consequently become a source of danger to the wearer.
items.value: 7.2.1 No person should put his rubber gauntlets or gloves into his coat or trouser pocket along with other tools. Gauntlets carried in this way are liable to get damaged and consequently become a source of danger to the wearer.

items.md: **7.2.2** After the rubber gauntlets and gloves have been in use, they should be carefully cleaned at once and stored in a suitable chalk in a suitable container. No tool or other material shall be stored in this container.
items.value: 7.2.2 After the rubber gauntlets and gloves have been in use, they should be carefully cleaned at once and stored in a suitable chalk in a suitable container. No tool or other material shall be stored in this container.

items.md: ## 7.3 Testing Rubber Gauntlets or Gloves
items.value: 7.3 Testing Rubber Gauntlets or Gloves

items.md: **7.3.1** Before a person puts on his rubber gauntlets or gloves to start work on a new job, he should check each one for cuts, cracks and weak spots by rolling it up tightly, beginning at the gauntlets end, and notice if any air escapes. This is usually called as ' air test '. Gauntlets or gloves which show visible cuts, cracks or weak spots or air leakage in this test shall not be used and shall be returned and a new pair obtained. The visual lamp test may also be adopted to determine the condition of gauntlets and gloves ( *see* IS : 4770-1968\* ).
items.value: 7.3.1 Before a person puts on his rubber gauntlets or gloves to start work on a new job, he should check each one for cuts, cracks and weak spots by rolling it up tightly, beginning at the gauntlets end, and notice if any air escapes. The visual lamp test may also be adopted to determine the condition of gauntlets and gloves ( see IS : 4770-1968* ).

items.md: **7.3.2** Every pair of rubber gauntlets and gloves shall be carefully examined on each occasion before and after the work by the person-in-charge of the work to ensure safety to the workmen. They should be tested for leakage current at frequent intervals in accordance with IS : 4770-1968\*.

items.md: ## 8. SAFETY PRACTICES
items.value: 8. SAFETY PRACTICES

items.md: **8.1** In all electrical work, it is very necessary that certain elementary safety practices are observed. It has been found that quite a large number of accidents occur due to the neglect of these practices. The details of such practices are given in **Appendix C**.
items.value: 8.1 In all electrical work, it is very necessary that certain elementary safety practices are observed. It has been found that quite a large number of accidents occur due to the neglect of these practices. The details of such practices are given in Appendix C.

items.md: ### 8.2 Fires and Fire Extinguishers
items.value: 8.2 Fires and Fire Extinguishers

items.md: **8.2.1** In the event of fire on electrical mains or apparatus, the effected parts shall immediately be isolated completely from its source of supply of electrical energy.
items.value: 8.2.1 In the event of fire on electrical mains or apparatus, the effected parts shall immediately be isolated completely from its source of supply of electrical energy.

items.md: **8.2.2** Fire extinguishers, which are not insulated, should never be employed in fighting fires near exposed live conductors. Only such fire extinguishers should be used on electrical mains and apparatus which are marked as suitable for the purpose. When using fire hose, it should be ensured that the jet of water does not come into contact with live conductors (*see* IS : 1646-1961\* ).
items.value: 8.2.2 Fire extinguishers, which are not insulated, should never be employed in fighting fires near exposed live conductors. Only such fire extinguishers should be used on electrical mains and apparatus which are marked as suitable for the purpose. When using fire hose, it should be ensured that the jet of water does not come into contact with live conductors (see IS : 1646-1961* ).

items.md: **8.2.3** It is dangerous to throw a stream of water, a wet blanket or a stream from ordinary soda-acid type extinguishers on live mains or apparatus. When found necessary to use them, have all the neighbouring mains or apparatus made dead. Carbon dioxide, carbon tetrachloride and other special type of extinguishers, sand or dry blanket may be used on live conductors and static apparatus.

items.md: # 9. TRAINING OF EMPLOYEES
items.value: 9. TRAINING OF EMPLOYEES

items.md: **9.1 General** — For maximum effectiveness, a sound safety procedure would include, in addition to the instructions on the hazards of electricity, thorough training of all employees who work on electrical installations and equipments. The employees should be trained in first aid, fire fighting, use of warning signs, guards and other protective devices and safe operational procedures.
items.value: 9.1 General — For maximum effectiveness, a sound safety procedure would include, in addition to the instructions on the hazards of electricity, thorough training of all employees who work on electrical installations and equipments. The employees should be trained in first aid, fire fighting, use of warning signs, guards and other protective devices and safe operational procedures.

items.md: **9.2 Training Programme** — The training programme should consist of not merely generalizations and indefinite recommendations, but should be specifically based upon demonstrations and training in the correct operation and maintenance of lines and apparatus.
items.value: 9.2 Training Programme — The training programme should consist of not merely generalizations and indefinite recommendations, but should be specifically based upon demonstrations and training in the correct operation and maintenance of lines and apparatus.

items.md: **9.3 Supervision**
items.value: 9.3 Supervision

items.md: **9.3.1** Supervising officers should maintain a close supervision over all operation and maintenance works and defective or careless handling of equipment should not be permitted.

items.md: **9.3.2** Under no circumstances should deviation from safe practices be tolerated, as this could result in unnecessary hazards and danger to workmen or apparatus.

items.md: **B-6.** Where work is to be carried out on live low or medium voltage mains or apparatus, the following additional instructions shall be complied with:
items.value: B-6. Where work is to be carried out on live low or medium voltage mains or apparatus, the following additional instructions shall be complied with:

items.md: a) No work shall be carried out by any person unless he is adequately protected from the risk of electric shock by the use of rubber gloves or other approved equipment specially provided for the purpose.
items.value: a) No work shall be carried out by any person unless he is adequately protected from the risk of electric shock by the use of rubber gloves or other approved equipment specially provided for the purpose.

items.md: b) When a person is working on live mains or apparatus, he shall always be accompanied by a second person who shall preferably be capable of rendering first aid and artificial respirations.
items.value: b) When a person is working on live mains or apparatus, he shall always be accompanied by a second person who shall preferably be capable of rendering first aid and artificial respirations.

items.md: c) When a man ascends a pole where the line is alive, he shall make use of a safety belt and rubber gloves; such work shall be carried out under the direction of an experienced person who is competent to supervise the specific work and who shall remain present in the immediate vicinity for the whole time the work is in progress.
items.value: c) When a man ascends a pole where the line is alive, he shall make use of a safety belt and rubber gloves; such work shall be carried out under the direction of an experienced person who is competent to supervise the specific work and who shall remain present in the immediate vicinity for the whole time the work is in progress.

items.md: d) The person-in-charge shall examine the safety equipment before use by the workmen to ensure that it is in sound condition, and also that it is being properly used.
items.value: d) The person-in-charge shall examine the safety equipment before use by the workmen to ensure that it is in sound condition, and also that it is being properly used.

items.md: **B-7.** On completion of the work, remove all earthing devices so that the mains and apparatus are fit in all respects for charging; withdraw all workmen and warn them that it is no longer safe to touch or approach the mains and apparatus, and thereafter return the keys and the permit duly discharged to the permit cancelling authority for cancellation.

items.md: **B-8.** All accidents shall be immediately attended to and reported to the proper authorities. In the event of a serious accident involving danger to life, the person-in-charge shall immediately get a doctor on site or remove the victim to the nearest hospital with the minimum delay. In the event of the victim being certified dead by a doctor, the body should NOT be removed without the permission of the police.

items.md: ( *Clause* 8.1 )
items.value: ( Clause 8.1 )

items.md: ## GENERAL SAFETY PRACTICES IN ELECTRICAL WORK
items.value: GENERAL SAFETY PRACTICES IN ELECTRICAL WORK

items.md: ### C-1. EXERCISE CARE
items.value: C-1. EXERCISE CARE

items.md: **C-1.1** Place yourself in a safe and secure position to avoid slipping, stumbling or moving backward against live conductors or apparatus. Do not rely for protection upon the care assumed to be exercised by others.
items.value: C-1.1 Place yourself in a safe and secure position to avoid slipping, stumbling or moving backward against live conductors or apparatus. Do not rely for protection upon the care assumed to be exercised by others.

items.md: **C-1.2** In the event of near approach of a lightning storm, all outdoor work on electrical system should cease.
items.value: C-1.2 In the event of near approach of a lightning storm, all outdoor work on electrical system should cease.

items.md: **C-1.3** Make a habit of being cautious. Be on the lookout for danger notice plates, danger flags, warning boards and signals, etc. Warn others when they seem to be in danger near live conductors or apparatus.
items.value: C-1.3 Make a habit of being cautious. Be on the lookout for danger notice plates, danger flags, warning boards and signals, etc. Warn others when they seem to be in danger near live conductors or apparatus.

items.md: ### C-2. PERSONAL APPAREL
items.value: C-2. PERSONAL APPAREL

items.md: **C-2.1** Use of overalls, dungarees, jumpers and coats having metal buttons, metal straps and similar metal fittings should be avoided. Bone buttons may be used. Buttons should be sewed in place with thread. Loose clothing should not be worn.
items.value: C-2.1 Use of overalls, dungarees, jumpers and coats having metal buttons, metal straps and similar metal fittings should be avoided. Bone buttons may be used. Buttons should be sewed in place with thread. Loose clothing should not be worn.

items.md: **C-2.2** While working on live conductors, do not roll up sleeves as dry cloth gives some protection against shocks.
items.value: C-2.2 While working on live conductors, do not roll up sleeves as dry cloth gives some protection against shocks.

items.md: **C-2.3** Do not wear shoes with nailed soles. Shoes should have sewn soles, or preferably rubber soles.
items.value: C-2.3 Do not wear shoes with nailed soles. Shoes should have sewn soles, or preferably rubber soles.

items.md: **C-2.4** Do not wear suspenders and arm bands with metal buckles or other metal parts. These might come in close proximity to live parts and cause serious, if not fatal, injury.
items.value: C-2.4 Do not wear suspenders and arm bands with metal buckles or other metal parts. These might come in close proximity to live parts and cause serious, if not fatal, injury.

items.md: **C-2.5** Metal key chains, or metal keepers for key rings or watch chains should not be worn on the outside of clothing. There is always a possibility that they may come in contact with live conductors or live apparatus.
items.value: C-2.5 Metal key chains, or metal keepers for key rings or watch chains should not be worn on the outside of clothing. There is always a possibility that they may come in contact with live conductors or live apparatus.

items.md: **C-2.6** While welding wear goggles, safety glasses or any other eye protection as instructed by the person-in-charge depending upon the type of work handled.
items.value: C-2.6 While welding wear goggles, safety glasses or any other eye protection as instructed by the person-in-charge depending upon the type of work handled.

### C-3. TREAT EVERYTHING AS LIVE
items.value: C-3. TREAT EVERYTHING AS LIVE

items.md: **C-3.1** Treat all electrical conductors and apparatus always as live and consequently dangerous to human life, unless it is positively know to be dead and properly earthed and take precautions accordingly.
items.value: C-3.1 Treat all electrical conductors and apparatus always as live and consequently dangerous to human life, unless it is positively know to be dead and properly earthed and take precautions accordingly.

items.md: ### C-4. THINK BEFORE YOU ACT
items.value: C-4. THINK BEFORE YOU ACT

items.md: **C-4.1** Think carefully before you act. Make sure you are right. Watch out for the other man and make sure he is right.
items.value: C-4.1 Think carefully before you act. Make sure you are right. Watch out for the other man and make sure he is right.

items.md: **C-4.2** Never speak to any person working upon live mains or apparatus, unless the person doing the work is aware of your presence.
items.value: C-4.2 Never speak to any person working upon live mains or apparatus, unless the person doing the work is aware of your presence.

items.md: ### C-5. DANGEROUS VOLTAGES
items.value: C-5. DANGEROUS VOLTAGES

items.md: **C-5.1** All voltages are dangerous. It shall be borne in mind that even low voltage shock may be fatal.
items.value: C-5.1 All voltages are dangerous. It shall be borne in mind that even low voltage shock may be fatal.

items.md: **C-5.2** Every person-in-charge of work on any mains or apparatus shall ensure himself that the same is free from dangerous leakage or induction and has been effectively earthed locally before permitting men to work upon it.
items.value: C-5.2 Every person-in-charge of work on any mains or apparatus shall ensure himself that the same is free from dangerous leakage or induction and has been effectively earthed locally before permitting men to work upon it.

items.md: ### C-6. DANGEROUS AREAS
items.value: C-6. DANGEROUS AREAS

items.md: **C-6.1** When working in areas which contain or may contain live mains and apparatus, fix danger notice plates, barriers, rails or other guarding arrangement for the working area. Do not store materials within high voltage enclosures or low voltage areas.
items.value: C-6.1 When working in areas which contain or may contain live mains and apparatus, fix danger notice plates, barriers, rails or other guarding arrangement for the working area. Do not store materials within high voltage enclosures or low voltage areas.

items.md: ### C-7. WARNING BOARDS
items.value: C-7. WARNING BOARDS

items.md: **C-7.1** Warning boards shall be placed by the person-in-charge on all switchgear before men are permitted to work and should only be removed by the person who has placed them. It is desirable that the person issuing the permit shall place one warning board on the switch energizing the mains for each permit issued so that he can be sure that all the permits-to-work are returned when he has to charge the mains.
items.value: C-7.1 Warning boards shall be placed by the person-in-charge on all switchgear before men are permitted to work and should only be removed by the person who has placed them. It is desirable that the person issuing the permit shall place one warning board on the switch energizing the mains for each permit issued so that he can be sure that all the permits-to-work are returned when he has to charge the mains.

items.md: ### C-8. VISITORS AND UNAUTHORIZED PERSONS
items.value: C-8. VISITORS AND UNAUTHORIZED PERSONS

items.md: **C-8.1** Visitors and unauthorized persons shall not be allowed to proceed in the vicinity of live mains and apparatus, unless accompained by an authorized person whose responsibility it shall be to ensure that his instructions regarding safety are strictly complied with.
items.value: C-8.1 Visitors and unauthorized persons shall not be allowed to proceed in the vicinity of live mains and apparatus, unless accompained by an authorized person whose responsibility it shall be to ensure that his instructions regarding safety are strictly complied with.

### C-9. WORKING IN DAMP SITUATIONS
items.value: C-9. WORKING IN DAMP SITUATIONS

items.md: **C-9.1** Extra precautions should be taken when working in abnormally damp area.
items.value: C-9.1 Extra precautions should be taken when working in abnormally damp area.

items.md: ### C-10. USE OF TONG OR CLIP-ON AMMETERS
items.value: C-10. USE OF TONG OR CLIP-ON AMMETERS

items.md: **C-10.1** These shall not be used on high voltage conductors, unless the conductors are lead-sheathed. On low voltage conductors, all persons, not accustomed to the handling of tong or clip-on ammeter, shall be warned of the necessary precautions to be observed before being allowed to use it.
items.value: C-10.1 These shall not be used on high voltage conductors, unless the conductors are lead-sheathed. On low voltage conductors, all persons, not accustomed to the handling of tong or clip-on ammeter, shall be warned of the necessary precautions to be observed before being allowed to use it.

items.md: ### C-11. WARNING THE PUBLIC
items.value: C-11. WARNING THE PUBLIC

items.md: **C-11.1** When, either accidentally or otherwise, live mains and apparatus constitute a danger to persons in a public place, a person shall be detailed to stand by and personally warn the public until the danger has been removed.
items.value: C-11.1 When, either accidentally or otherwise, live mains and apparatus constitute a danger to persons in a public place, a person shall be detailed to stand by and personally warn the public until the danger has been removed.

items.md: ### C-12. PORTABLE ELECTRICAL APPARATUS
items.value: C-12. PORTABLE ELECTRICAL APPARATUS

items.md: **C-12.1** All portable electrical apparatus shall be regularly examined, tested and maintained to ensure that the apparatus and leads are in good order.
items.value: C-12.1 All portable electrical apparatus shall be regularly examined, tested and maintained to ensure that the apparatus and leads are in good order.

items.md: **C-12.2** Ensure that all portable appliances are provided with 3-pin plug and socket connections and that the metal work of the apparatus is effectively earthed.
items.value: C-12.2 Ensure that all portable appliances are provided with 3-pin plug and socket connections and that the metal work of the apparatus is effectively earthed.

items.md: **C-12.3** All loose wiring, such as flexible cables for portable lamps, tools and trailing cables and other portable and transportable apparatus, shall be tested regularly at frequent intervals to ensure safety.
items.value: C-12.3 All loose wiring, such as flexible cables for portable lamps, tools and trailing cables and other portable and transportable apparatus, shall be tested regularly at frequent intervals to ensure safety.
